result.stdout:
dotnet-wait v1.0.0.0 - List wait duration
by Christophe Nasarre

Missing pid or app to execute/resume
Usage:  dotnet-wait  -w <min wait duration threshold>  -o <file to save the waits details>  
             -p <process ID> 
             -s  -- <path to an app to spawn followed by its command line if needed>
             -r <pid of the instance to use with -s> 
        If the process is already running, use -p <pid>.
        To spawn a new process, use -- <executable path>
        Use -s when you need to start the child process in the current console and get the wait input/output in another one
        when you use -r followed by the pid of the process using -s
Ex: -p 1234            list all waits for running process 1234
    -w 100 -p 1234     list waits lasting more than 100 ms for process 1234
    -o waits.txt -- dotnet foo.dll arg1 "a r g 2"     start foo.dll with arguments and save the wait details in waits.txt  !!! inputs are shared between the tool and foo !!!
    -s -- dotnet foo.dll arg1 "a r g 2"   start foo.dll with arguments and wait for the following command line to be run in another console
    -r 2468                                 resume foo.dll and output wait details/input in the current console. Note: 2468 is given by the -s command
